title: olearia virgata subsp. serpentina
intro: Olearia virgata subsp. serpentina, also known as the narrow-leaved daisy bush, is a shrub native to Australia that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Olearia virgata subsp. serpentina include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor injuries. In terms of herbal preparations, Olearia virgata subsp. serpentina can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for a variety of applications to harness its health benefits.
